cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure this out. So the question is about Jane's birthday and calculating a date 10 days before her 16th birthday. First, Jane was born on the last day of February in 2001. Wait, the last day of February is usually the 28th, unless it's a leap year when it's the 29th. But 2001 isn't a leap year. Leap years are divisible by 4, so 2000 was a leap year, then 2004. So 2001 is not a leap year. Therefore, Jane's birthday is February 28, 2001. Today is her 16th birthday. So her 16th birthday would be in 2017, right? Because 2001 plus 16 years is 2017. So her birthday in 2017 would be February 28, 2017. So today is February 28, 2017. Now, the question is asking what the date was 10 days ago. So if today is February 28, 2017, subtract 10 days. Let's count backwards. February 28 minus 10 days. Let me think. February has 28 days in 2017 (not a leap year). So if we go back 10 days from February 28: February 28 - 1 day = February 27 - 2 days = Feb 26 ... continuing that... - 10 days would be February 18, 2017. Because 28 minus 10 is 18. But wait, is there anything about the year changing? No, because it's still within February. So 10 days before February 28 is February 18. So the answer would be 02/18/2017, which is option B. Let me check the options again. Options: A) 02/28/2017 (that would be her birthday, not 10 days ago) B) 02/18/2017 (this is 10 days earlier in the same month) C) 03/18/2017 (March is after February, so no) D) 02/13/2017 (only 15 days earlier) E) 02/25/2017 (only 3 days earlier) F) 03/04/2017 (March again, so no) Wait, but let me double-check my math. Let me count the days step by step. Starting from February 28, 2017, and going back 10 days: 1 day before: Feb 27 2: Feb 26 3: Feb 25 4: Feb 24 5: Feb 23 6: Feb 22 7: Feb 21 8: Feb 20 9: Feb 19 10: Feb 18 Yes, so 10 days before February 28 is February 18.
